hub_subtitle: Inglês para Trabalho Remoto em Tecnologia & Quality Assurance

lesson.menu_title: Your Career Story -- Talking About Your Experience
lesson.menu_desc: Telling your professional story in a remote interview -- 33 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 2 -- Your Career Story
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Ana Claudia Veraldi | Lesson 2 -- Your Career Story
lesson.grammar_point: present perfect with for/since and ever/never

vocab.title: Match each word to its meaning
defs: the series of jobs and roles across your working life
an important achievement that marks real progress
a move to a higher, more senior position
your past studies, training, and work experience
deep skill and knowledge in one specific field
a demanding task that tests your ability
to reach a goal after real effort
to focus your work on one particular area

paras: Ana is a systems analyst, and she has built a strong career path in technology. She has worked in quality assurance since 2018, and she has been in tech for more than ten years. Her background is in computer science. Testing is her area of expertise: she has specialized in finding critical problems before software reaches the user.

Ana has achieved a lot. She has earned two promotions, and she has led a small QA team since last year. She has mentored several junior testers, and that is one of her favorite milestones. Every project brings a new challenge, but she has never missed an important deadline. She has always been curious about the world, but she has never worked with a fully international team. That is her next goal: she wants to tell her career story in English and find remote work abroad.

To talk about your experience up to now, use the present perfect: have or has plus the past participle. Use 'for' with a period of time (for ten years) and 'since' with a starting point (since 2018). Use 'ever' in questions (Have you ever led a team?) and 'never' for no experience (I have never worked abroad).
reading.source: Alumni by Better -- B2 reading, adapted from a professional profile

reading.prompt: What is the main idea of the text?
choices: Ana is describing one project she finished last week.
Ana is telling her career story so far, and we use the present perfect to talk about experience up to now.
The text explains how to write software in computer science.

items: Ana has worked in quality assurance since 2018.
The text says: 'She has worked in quality assurance since 2018.'
Ana has already worked with a fully international team.
The text says: 'She has never worked with a fully international team.'
The text says: 'She has earned two promotions.'
Ana has often missed important deadlines.
The text says: 'She has never missed an important deadline.'

gapfill.text: Remember: present perfect = have/has + past participle. Use 'for' with a period (for six years) and 'since' with a starting point (since 2018). Use 'never' for no experience (I have never worked abroad).

bank.label: Useful sentences to tell your career story
items: I have worked in quality assurance for six years.
I have been in tech since 2018.
I have specialized in software testing.
I have led a small QA team since last year.
I have mentored several junior testers.
I have never worked with an international team.
Have you ever led a project? Yes, I have.

lf.text: 'for' + a period of time (for six years). 'since' + a starting point (since 2018). 'ever' goes in questions (Have you ever...?). 'never' means no experience up to now (I have never...).

items.situation: A recruiter asks: 'How long have you worked in QA?' Answer in one sentence.
tips: I have worked in QA for six years.
I have been in QA since 2018.

items.situation: Say one thing you have never done at work, using 'never'.
tips: I have never led a team of ten people.

items.situation: Ask your interviewer a question with 'Have you ever...?'.
tips: Have you ever worked with a remote QA team?
Have you ever hired someone from Brazil?

items.situation: Describe one milestone you have achieved, using the present perfect.
tips: I have mentored several junior testers.

items: Tell your career story in three sentences: how long you have worked in QA, your area of expertise, and one milestone you have achieved. Use your notes.
Say it again, and add one sentence with 'for' and one with 'since': 'I have worked in QA for six years. I have led a team since last year.'
Now do it with no notes. Sound natural, not memorized.
The recruiter asks: 'Have you ever worked with an international team?' Answer honestly with the present perfect: 'No, I haven't, but I have always wanted to.'

answerkey.title: Reveal the model career story
list: Model: 'I am a systems analyst. I have worked in quality assurance for six years, and I have been in tech since 2018. My expertise is software testing. I have earned two promotions, and I have led a small QA team since last year. I have never worked with an international team, but I have always wanted to.'
Duration up to now: for + period (for six years) / since + starting point (since 2018).
Experience: Have you ever...? / I have never... / I have always...
Form: have or has + past participle (worked, led, earned, mentored).
answerkey.note: Use the present perfect for experience and duration up to now. Use the past simple only for a finished time (I worked at WIPRO in 2016).

listenings.text: Hi, I am Ana, and this is a short version of my career story. I have worked in quality assurance for more than six years, and I have been in technology since 2018. My background is in computer science. I have specialized in software testing, and testing is my area of expertise. Over the years, I have earned two promotions, and I have led a small QA team since last year. I have also mentored several junior testers. That is one of my favorite milestones. I have never worked with a fully international team, but that is exactly what I am looking for now. Thank you for listening.

Hi Ana, this is David Harper from the hiring team at Northwind. Thank you for your application for the remote QA Analyst role. I have reviewed your profile, and your experience looks strong. Before our interview on Thursday, I would like you to prepare a short version of your career story. Please tell me three things: how long you have worked in quality assurance, what your area of expertise is, and one milestone you have achieved. Keep it clear and use the present perfect. I am looking forward to hearing it. Talk soon.

extra_audio.text: First, David thanks Ana for her application and introduces the role. Then he asks how long she has worked in quality assurance. Next, Ana explains her experience and her area of expertise. After that, David asks if she has ever worked with an international team. Finally, they agree to continue the interview on Thursday.
